words.meaning: To move from one place to another
words.exampleSentence: I go to school every day.

words.meaning: To move towards a person or place
words.exampleSentence: Please come here.

words.meaning: To put food into the mouth and swallow it
words.exampleSentence: I eat rice for lunch.

words.meaning: To take liquid into the mouth and swallow it
words.exampleSentence: Drink plenty of water.

words.meaning: To look at and understand written words
words.exampleSentence: She likes to read storybooks.

words.meaning: To mark letters or words on paper
words.exampleSentence: I write a letter to my friend.

words.meaning: To say something to express thoughts
words.exampleSentence: He can speak English well.

words.meaning: To give attention to a sound
words.exampleSentence: Listen to your teacher carefully.

words.meaning: To rest with eyes closed
words.exampleSentence: I sleep eight hours every night.

words.meaning: To move fast on feet
words.exampleSentence: The boy can run very fast.

words.meaning: To move by taking steps
words.exampleSentence: We walk in the park every morning.

words.meaning: To do activities for fun
words.exampleSentence: They play football in the afternoon.

words.meaning: To use eyes to look at something
words.exampleSentence: I can see a bird on the tree.

words.meaning: To perceive sound with ears
words.exampleSentence: I hear a strange noise.

words.meaning: To hand over something to someone
words.exampleSentence: Give me your pen, please.

words.meaning: To reach out and get something
words.exampleSentence: Take your umbrella with you.

words.meaning: To get something by paying money
words.exampleSentence: I want to buy a new shirt.

words.meaning: To give something in exchange for money
words.exampleSentence: He sells vegetables in the market.

words.meaning: To move something to allow access
words.exampleSentence: Please open the door.

words.meaning: To move something to cover an opening
words.exampleSentence: Close the window, it is cold.
